plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Reduce exposure to semiconductors (SMH) and high-growth tech (CRWD).
Trim broad index (SPY) to lower concentration and free up capital.
Buy into the defensive healthcare sector (XLV) for stability.
Increase allocation to income-focused QQQI for steadier returns.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation and low drawdowns, targeting around 1.5% monthly. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leverage and concentration, building a cash buffer implicitly through reduced exposure.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and eliminate others (SPAL) to reduce risk.
Reduce concentration in broad index (SPY) and semiconductors (SMH).
Maintain exposure to quality tech (CRWD) and add new quality names (CRM).
Introduce defensive healthcare (XLV) for sector diversification.
This balanced approach aims for risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% monthly.
It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and defensive elements.
balanced.read: This plan seeks risk-first growth, targeting approximately 2.5% monthly. It balances reducing concentration and leverage with adding quality and defensive names, aiming for moderate but consistent gains with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Increase exposure to leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and semiconductors (SMH).
Concentrate further in high-scoring tech (CRWD) and add new top setups (NET, NOW).
Trim broad index (SPY) to fund these aggressive additions.
This plan leverages conviction in strong performers and high-potential names.
It aims for aggressive growth, targeting around 4% monthly, but with higher potential drawdowns.
Strict stop discipline is critical due to amplified risk and leveraged ETF decay.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets around 4% monthly, accepting significantly higher potential drawdowns and leverage risk. It concentrates capital in the strongest performing tech and semiconductor names, including leveraged ETFs, with a smaller cash buffer and the expectation of amplified gains and losses.

plan: Sell all volatile single stocks and leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL, SMH, CRWD).
Trim broad index (SPY) to reduce growth focus and increase cash.
Maximize allocation to income-focused QQQI for steady distributions.
Add defensive healthcare (XLV) for additional stable income potential.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reliable monthly income.
Targeting around 1% monthly from distributions, with low expected drawdowns.
income.read: This income-focused plan prioritizes capital preservation and steady monthly distributions, targeting around 1% monthly. It eliminates volatile growth assets, concentrating in income-generating ETFs like QQQI and defensive sectors like XLV, aiming for low drawdowns and reliable cash flow.
